input.prompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

data.content:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-14T00:56:05.956Z</current_datetime>

I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

arguments.command: mkdir -p McpServer && cd McpServer && dotnet new console --force -n McpServer --output . 2>&1 | tail -3

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server project with:
1. A tool class wrapping a REST API using HttpClient
2. Program.cs with stdio transport
3. An explanation of how DI (Dependency Injection) works for MCP tools

Let me provide a complete example. I'll create the files in the current working directory.

arguments.command: mkdir -p McpServer && cd McpServer && dotnet new console --force -n McpServer --output . 2>&1 | tail -3

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "mkdir -p McpServer && cd McpServer && dotnet new console --force -n McpServer --output . 2>&1 | tail -3", "description": "Create new console project", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.command: mkdir -p McpServer && cd McpServer && dotnet new console --force -n McpServer --output . 2>&1 | tail -3

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http 2>&1 | tail -3
arguments.description: Add MCP SDK and DI packages

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Add MCP SDK and DI packages

arguments.command: cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http 2>&1 | tail -3
arguments.description: Add MCP SDK and DI packages

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http 2>&1 | tail -3", "description": "Add MCP SDK and DI packages", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.command: cd McpServer && dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 2>&1 | tail -3 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Http 2>&1 | tail -3
toolArgs.description: Add MCP SDK and DI packages
